token.bp_desc: WIM UI supports responsive design. The layout switches based on the following breakpoints.
token.bp_down_desc: Used to apply styles only below (max-width) the specified breakpoint.
token.bp_scss_desc: These tokens are defined as a map variable `$breakpoints` in `src/tokens/_breakpoints.scss`, and mixins to easily write media queries are provided.
token.bp_sm_device: Large smartphones or small tablets in portrait mode
token.bp_up_desc: Used in a 'mobile-first' layout to apply styles above (min-width) the specified breakpoint.
token.bp_mq_vs_cq_desc: WIM UI uses both media queries and container queries depending on the context. The key question is: does the component respond to the viewport width, or to the width of the container it is placed in?
token.bp_mq_criterion: The component itself defines page structure, or always occupies the full viewport width
token.bp_cq_criterion: The component is placed inside a layout and does not know how wide its container will be
token.bp_mq_examples: AppShell (sidebar show/hide), Dialog, BottomSheet (rendered in portal), global spacing scale, device feature detection (prefers-color-scheme, prefers-reduced-motion)
token.bp_cq_examples: QueryBuilder, BentoGrid, and other UI components that can be placed anywhere in a layout
token.bp_cq_why_title: Why container queries for UI components?
token.bp_cq_why_desc: When a component like QueryBuilder is used in a main area beside a sidebar, the viewport may be 1200px wide while the actual available width is only 600px. A media query at 768px would never trigger, causing the layout to overflow. Container queries respond to the actual rendered width of the component's container, making Storybook viewport tests accurately reflect real-world usage.
token.bp_cq_usage_desc: Add container-type: inline-size to the component's root element. Then use @container rules to target its descendants.
token.bp_cq_note: Container queries are supported in all modern browsers (Chrome 105+, Safari 16+, Firefox 110+).
token.color_checker_desc: Checks the contrast ratio between background and text colors to ensure accessibility standards are met.
token.color_ct_desc: Colors for text or icons placed on semantic color backgrounds.
token.type_alias_desc: To apply consistent styling across components, aliases with the `--wim-` prefix are provided for the global tokens above. It is recommended to use these direct alias variables when developing or customizing WIM UI components.
token.type_desc1: Defines font sizes, weights, and line heights for consistent typography. Font sizes follow a modular scale with a 1.125 (Major Second) ratio.
token.type_desc2: The scale ratio is managed by the `$font-scale-ratio` variable in `src/tokens/_spacings.scss`. Changing this value adjusts the font ratio across the system.
token.type_ff_desc: Font specifications for each language used throughout the application. The appropriate font is automatically applied according to the `lang` attribute of the html/body tag.
token.type_lh_jpan_loose:
Loose (1.8)
Easy to read in Japanese text, especially on mobile devices and long body text. Gives a relaxed impression and makes eye movement smooth.
token.type_lh_jpan_normal:
Normal (1.6)
The standard line height for WIM UI. Offers the best balance between readability and information density.
token.type_lh_jpan_tight:
Tight (1.4)
Used when you want to increase information density, such as headings and labels.
token.type_lh_latn_loose:
Loose (1.6)
The loose line height for Latin scripts provides ample breathing room between lines. It is ideal for legibility in long-form content.
token.type_lh_latn_normal:
Normal (1.4)
Normal line height is the standard for most application UI elements. It offers a balanced density that works well for descriptions.
token.type_lh_latn_tight:
Tight (1.2)
Tight line height is specifically designed for headings. It maintains strong visual proximity for related text elements.
token.type_text_ex_large:
Extra Large Text (20.25px)
Uses the 1.125 squared ratio ($font-size-2).
token.type_text_ex_small:
Extra Small Text (12.64px)
Uses the 1.125 power of -2 ratio ($font-size-minus-2).
token.type_text_small:
Small Text (14.22px)
Uses the 1.125 power of -1 ratio ($font-size-minus-1).
token.overview_desc: This is a list of design tokens that form the foundation of style definitions for WIM UI. It is recommended to use these variables to maintain a consistent design.

welcome: Welcome to the wimui Design System

dashboard.intro: You can check the development status and specification overview of all WIM UI components.
dashboard.tokens_link: For design foundations (colors, typography, etc.), please refer to the Design Token Overview.
